content_of_post_to_comment:
I’m a 19-year-old student currently struggling deeply with self-discipline and consistency.

Whenever I try to build a new routine—whether it’s sticking to my studies or building healthy habits—I can usually maintain it for the first week. But as soon as the second week hits, I lose momentum, start procrastinating, and completely fall off track.

Honestly, I wasn’t like this before. When I was 18 and younger, I was full of energy, excited about life, and naturally driven. Ever since I turned 19, things changed significantly. I spend almost my entire day glued to my phone, got into my first serious relationship, and completely slacked off on my education.

My daily screen time is getting out of hand, and I feel like my attention span and motivation are totally shot. I’m really not satisfied with where I am right now, and I want to fix this before it gets worse.

Specifically, I’d love to know:
1 How do you rebuild momentum when you've fallen into a cycle of heavy phone addiction and laziness?

2 What practical techniques helped you stay consistent past the first week?

3 How do you balance personal life, relationships, and studies without losing focus on your goals?

Any insights, advice, or book recommendations would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!
comment: The fact that you can hold a routine for a week means the problem probably isn’t discipline itself, but the system around it. I’d try making your phone less available before trying to become more motivated. Also, don’t see your relationship as the enemy; learning to protect study time while still enjoying life is part of becoming consistent.
